channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Greece hotel pools set to switch to seawater on drought-hit islands
item.description: ATHENS, Greece (AP) — Hotels on Greek islands may be filling their swimming pools with seawater this summer in response to worsening drought conditions, under new legislation being debated in parliament.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: A look at the terms -- and tensions -- in the Israel-Hamas draft ceasefire deal
item.description: If the Israel-Hamas ceasefire deal goes according to the current draft, then fighting will stop in Gaza for 42 days, and dozens of Israeli hostages and hundreds of Palestinian prisoners will be freed.
item.dc:creator: Lee Keath And Samy Magdy, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Hamas has accepted draft agreement for Gaza cease-fire, hostage release: officials
item.description: Hamas has reportedly agreed to a draft of a multi-phase cease-fire deal with Israel that would include the release of hostages who were taken into Gaza on Oct. 7, 2023.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Malaysia is home to the world's largest hotel, with over 7K rooms for guests
item.description: The Guinness World Record for the largest hotel by number of rooms goes to First World Hotel in Malaysia. The hotel boasts over 7,000 hotel rooms for guests to lounge in.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: ICC chief prosecutor wants Israeli objections over Netanyahu warrant to be rejected
item.description: THE HAGUE, Netherlands (AP) — The International Criminal Court’s chief prosecutor has told judges that Israeli objections to the investigation into the 13-month war in Gaza should be rejected.
item.dc:creator: MOLLY QUELL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: What to make of the final polling of the Biden years
item.description: A January 10-11 survey of 1,000 registered voters found President Joe Biden with a 37% approval rating in the final week of his presidency, 12 points lower than when Emerson first polled his approval in 2021.
item.dc:creator: Bill Disbrow, NewsNation

item.title: France’s new prime minister says he'll renegotiate a contested plan to raise the retirement age
item.description: France’s new prime minister, François Bayrou, has announced the renegotiation of a contested plan raising the retirement age from 62 to 64.
item.dc:creator: Sylvie Corbet, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: North Korea launches missiles toward eastern waters in 2nd launch of year, South says
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — North Korea on Tuesday test-fired multiple missiles toward its eastern waters, South Korea’s military said, as it continued its weapons demonstrations ahead of Donald Trump’s return to the White House.
item.dc:creator: KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Hamas has accepted a draft agreement for a Gaza ceasefire and the release of hostages, officials say
item.description: Officials say Hamas has accepted a draft agreement for a ceasefire in the Gaza Strip and the release of dozens of hostages.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Middle East latest: Gaza ceasefire talks press on in Qatar
item.description: Israel and Hamas appear to be inching closer to a phased ceasefire agreement, officials said Monday. However, a deal hasn't been reached yet and there are a number of obstacles that could still scupper the talks in Qatar. Israel's war against Hamas in Gaza began on Oct.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: No. 2 Senate Democratic leader will back Rubio for secretary of State
item.description: Senate Democratic Whip Dick Durbin (Ill.) on Monday announced that he will vote for Sen. Marco Rubio (R-Fla.) to become the next secretary of State, a sign that Rubio is on a path to be confirmed on President-elect Trump’s first day of office with overwhelming support.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, The Hill

item.title: Cease-fire between Israel and Hamas getting closer amid concerns terror group rearming in Gaza
item.description: Israel and Hamas are nearing a cease-fire deal amid escalating death toll; 5 IDF soldiers were killed in Gaza on Monday amid growing warnings that Hamas is rearming.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: At least 100 illegal miners have died while trapped in a South African mine for months, group says
item.description: JOHANNESBURG (AP) — At least 100 men who were mining illegally in an abandoned gold mine in South Africa have died of suspected starvation and dehydration after being trapped deep underground for months while police tried to force them out, a group representing the miners said Monday.
item.dc:creator: MOGOMOTSI MAGOME and GERALD IMRAY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Significant progress made in talks over Israel-Hamas ceasefire and hostage release, officials say
item.description: CAIRO (AP) — U.S. and Arab mediators made significant progress overnight toward brokering a ceasefire in the Israel-Hamas war and the release of scores of hostages held in the Gaza Strip, but a deal hasn't been reached yet, officials said Monday.
item.dc:creator: VICTORIA EASTWOOD, SAMY MAGDY and JOSEF FEDERMAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Italian photographer Oliviero Toscani, famed for provocative Benetton campaigns, dies at 82
item.description: MILAN (AP) — Oliviero Toscani, the photographer behind Benetton’s provocative ad campaigns of the 1980s and 1990s who later broke with the Italian knitwear brand amid controversy, died Monday at age 82. Toscani disclosed last year he had a rare disease.
item.dc:creator: COLLEEN BARRY and GIADA ZAMPANO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Watch live: Biden delivers foreign address at State Department
item.description: President Biden began his farewell tour around Washington this week with an address on Monday at the State Department to highlight his work on foreign policy during his presidency. Biden was expected to defend his foreign policy legacy, including reconnecting the U.S.
item.dc:creator: The Hill

item.title: Russian forces bypass a key stronghold in a bid to cut off its supplies, a Ukrainian officer says
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Russian forces are bypassing a key stronghold in eastern Ukraine that they have fought for months to capture and are focusing instead on cutting supply lines to it, a Ukrainian official said Monday.
item.dc:creator: SAMYA KULLAB and YEHOR KONOVALOV, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump, Putin call expected soon, Waltz says
item.description: President-elect Trump and Russian President Vladimir Putin are expected to have a phone call in the coming days or weeks, Trump’s incoming national security adviser, Rep. Mike Waltz (R-Fla.), said Sunday.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, The Hill

item.title: Biden takes victory lap in final foreign policy speech
item.description: Biden is expected to discuss wars in Ukraine, Middle East and aggression from China, Iran and North Korea.
item.dc:creator: Tom Dempsey, NewsNation

item.title: China's exports in December up 10.7%, beating estimates as higher US tariffs loom
item.description: HONG KONG (AP) — China’s exports in December grew at a faster pace than expected, as factories rushed to fill orders to beat higher tariffs that U.S. President-elect Donald Trump has threatened to impose once he takes office.
item.dc:creator: ZEN SOO and ELAINE KURTENBACH, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: 1 poll finds majority of Greenland respondents support joining US
item.description: A new survey found that a majority of Greenland respondents support joining the United States. According to a poll by Patriot Polling released Sunday, 57.3 percent of respondents approve of Greenland becoming part of the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, The Hill

item.title: Trump's talk of expansion puts world leaders on alert
item.description: President-elect Trump’s talk of territorial expansion has rattled world leaders at an already precarious time in global politics.
item.dc:creator: Julia Mueller, NewsNation

item.title: Russia says it will continue oil and gas projects despite US sanctions
item.description: Russia's Foreign Ministry has denounced U.S. sanctions against Moscow's energy sector and said the country would carry on with large oil and gas projects.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump issues warning to Maduro as Venezuelan leader enters third term, US expands sanctions
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump issued a warning to Venezuelan President Nicolas Maduro, who entered his third term Friday, that he better keep democracy activists 'safe and alive' after detaining Maria Machado.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump setting up meeting with Putin, in communication with Xi
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump said he is working on setting up a meeting with Russian President Vladimir Putin for after his Jan. 20 inauguration and is in "communication" with Chinese President Xi Jinping.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump's victory could give boost to Brazil's Bolsonaro's re-election hopes amid court ban on him running
item.description: Brazil's supreme court has prevented Jair Bolsonaro from running again in the 2026 presidential election, yet the former president and his team are hoping the incoming Trump administration might help.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Thousands of Venezuelan opposition supporters take to the streets ahead of Maduro's third inauguration
item.description: Venezuelan opposition supporters protested around the country in a last-minute effort to put pressure on President Maduro the day before he is to be sworn in for his third term.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Cold spell claims first two lives in El Paso-Juarez area
item.description: Tuesday’s death of a homeless man presumably due to hypothermia has set off alarms in a border city with a large floating population of migrants.
item.dc:creator: Julian Resendiz, NewsNation
